findings.title: Persona and delegation model are tightly aligned
findings.detail: Irene is defined as a senior instructional designer who does not write prose; capabilities, Principles, and External Agents consistently delegate drafting to Paige, Sophia, and Caravaggio while Irene owns pedagogy, briefs, review, and assembly. This resolves the common failure mode of a single agent both critiquing and producing copy.

findings.title: Progressive disclosure maps capabilities to reference files
findings.detail: Internal capability codes (IA, LO, BT, CL, CS, AA, PQ, WD, SM) route to pedagogical-framework, delegation-protocol, templates, and save-memory. External agents are listed with status and context passed, which supports predictable orchestration from Marcus.

findings.title: Writer selection matrix closes persona–capability loop
findings.detail: The matrix ties content type and Bloom's level to a specific writer with rationale, matching Irene's stated role of telling each writer exactly what to write and why. Editorial review steps are sequenced before pedagogical quality review.

findings.title: Single pedagogical core supports multiple internal codes
findings.detail: Bloom, cognitive load, backward design, sequencing, and instructional analysis live in one reference, avoiding contradictory guidance across IA/LO/BT/CL/CS.

findings.title: Interactive mode is thin relative to headless delegation
findings.detail: Headless flow is specified in detail (context envelope, validation, style bible, return payload). Interactive mode is a one-line greeting pattern with no branching, prioritization, or session structure. Users who invoke Irene without Marcus may lack a clear end-to-end path comparable to delegation.
findings.action: Add a short interactive subsection: suggested prompts, how to elicit objectives if missing, and when to hand off to Marcus.

findings.title: No explicit capability row for template-driven assembly
findings.detail: Six artifact types and templates are documented, but assembly into lesson_plan | narration_script | etc. is implied under workflow rather than named as a capability. Operators may not map 'what Irene does last' to a labeled step.
findings.action: Optionally add an internal code (e.g., AS — Artifact assembly) pointing to template paths and pairing invariants, or state explicitly under WD that assembly is part of WD.

findings.title: Sidecar files referenced but not created by init.md
findings.detail: memory-system.md describes index, access-boundaries, patterns, chronology; init.md lists initial creation including access-boundaries.md. If Irene follows only init on first run, behavior matches. If index is created without access-boundaries, boundary rules in memory-system are harder to enforce consistently.
findings.action: Ensure init.md explicitly mirrors memory-system file list or cross-link 'must create access-boundaries.md before writes'.

findings.title: Five capability codes share one pedagogical reference file
findings.detail: IA, LO, BT, CL, CS all load pedagogical-framework.md. This is documentation routing, not duplicate content, but the table can read like five separate tools when operationally it is one framework with multiple lenses.
findings.action: In SKILL.md, add one line under Internal Capabilities that IA–CS are facets of the same framework to reduce perceived redundancy.

findings.title: Backward design repeated across SKILL, framework, and assessment template
findings.detail: Backward design appears in Principles, pedagogical-framework, and template-assessment-brief. Reinforcement aids consistency; token cost is moderate.
findings.action: Keep as-is for reinforcement, or add a single 'see pedagogical-framework' pointer in the assessment template header to trim repetition if token budget matters.

findings.title: Quality boundaries: PQ vs Quality Reviewer vs editorial
findings.detail: Does Not says Quality Reviewer handles validation; delegation-protocol requires editorial prose/structure and PQ pedagogical checks. This is coherent if QR is clinical/QA and Irene is pedagogical, but the SKILL could state explicitly that editorial + PQ are not substitutes for the Quality Reviewer.
findings.action: Add one sentence under Does Not or External Agents clarifying QR scope versus editorial and PQ.

findings.title: Save Memory (SM) is minimal versus Principle 8 learning loop
findings.detail: Principle 8 and memory-system describe rich patterns/chronology; save-memory.md is a short stub. Cohesion between promised learning loop and executable steps is weaker than for delegation.
findings.action: Expand save-memory.md with triggers aligned to memory-system (delegation complete, approval, run end) and what fields to update in index vs patterns vs chronology.

findings.title: Optional explicit style-bible capability
findings.detail: Style bible is mandatory on activation and in degradation handling but is not a named internal code. Naming it would align the capability table with On Activation and Principles.
findings.action: Add SB — Style bible consultation (read-only, fresh each task) referencing resources/style-bible/.

agent_identity.name: bmad-agent-content-creator
agent_identity.persona_summary: Irene — senior instructional architect for medical education; owns learning science, delegation briefs, pedagogical review, and structured artifacts; does not author prose.
agent_identity.primary_purpose: Design and sequence pedagogically grounded content, delegate drafting to specialist writers, run editorial and pedagogical review, emit six artifact types with downstream consumption notes for production specialists.

persona_alignment.notes: Expertise (Bloom, cognitive load, backward design) matches internal references; non-writing stance matches writer and editorial delegation; communication style in SKILL matches precision and pedagogical justification.

missing_areas: Stronger SM/save-memory detail vs memory-system ambition
Explicit QR vs editorial vs PQ boundary sentence
capability_completeness.notes: Core headless journey from Marcus through writers to staging paths is covered; first-time direct users and memory write discipline are the main softness.

consolidation_opportunities.suggested_consolidation: Treat as one pedagogical core in UX copy while keeping codes for traceability if needed.
Cross-reference once if trimming tokens without losing reinforcement.

external_integration.notes: Writer matrix maps content types to Paige, Sophia, Caravaggio; editorial passes are ordered before PQ; downstream humans/tools (Gary, ElevenLabs, Qualtrics) are consumption-only, which matches Does Not.

user_journey_score.notes: Marcus-delegated production is coherent end-to-end. Direct interactive use and first-run without full course_context rely more on LLM improvisation than on scripted steps.

summary.single_most_important_fix: Expand save-memory.md and align it with memory-system and Principle 8 so the learning loop is as actionable as the delegation protocol.
summary.assessment: Irene is a well-integrated instructional-delegation agent: persona, writers, templates, and downstream annotations align. Minor gaps are interactive depth, explicit assembly labeling, memory-save detail, and a clearer line between pedagogical review and the Quality Reviewer.
